x.name: Andrej Karpathy
x.bio: I like training large deep neural nets.

tweets.text:
This is a new paradigm for interacting with Claude that is significantly more "inline" with all the other human activity org-wide. Once you do all of the under the hood engineering work to make this "just work" (e.g. across tools, integrations, compute environments, memory, security, etc.), Claude basically joins the team in a seamless way - you can talk to it as you would talk to a person and it can help with a very large variety of workloads.

Imo this is the 3rd major redesign of LLM UIUX. The first paradigm was that the LLM is a website you go to, the second was that it is an app you download to your computer. This third one is that it is a self-contained, persistent, asynchronous entity with org-wide tools and context, working alongside teams of humans. It really takes a while to wrap your head around it, but it works and it is awesome.

Florida State University: "Shortly after introducing NotebookLM on campus, we watched students who were struggling with a ‘C’ grade completely transform their study habits and their grades in a matter of weeks."

https://t.co/xcPbvA37s0

That's it. Point it at a channel, give it a task, and let it work. It's in beta on Slack today for Claude Enterprise and Team customers. More surfaces coming soon!

Learn more: https://t.co/tcvNSCANlf

Tag Claude in a channel, it spins up an instance with its own sandbox. It clones repos, writes code, tests, compiles all in that isolated environment and the sandbox gets thrown away when it's done. One instance per thread, its own memory and permissions per channel.

I also have Claude monitoring Slack channels to proactively respond to people to answer their questions and draft PRs, and to react with specific emojis like ✅❌ when a thread is resolved.

To set it up, just add @.Claude to a channel and ask it to do this^, in natural language.

x.name: Madhu Guru
x.bio: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.

tweets.text:
I’ve had the chance to see this ecosystem from the different sides - enterprises, agent startups, data providers, labs. 

The core tension here is that the business models, moats, value exchange, execution playbooks for all players are being figured out in real time.

This debate around token pricing is really a debate over where value will accrue - models, application layer, distribution or other layers.

Expect a lot of strong opinions from the different sides to shape the narrative in their favor.

There are 100s of ways you can customize Claude Tag for any use case.

Here are 6 common flows that have resonated with our internal users and external design partners: https://t.co/eMw8xdDZOZ
Here’s our Get Started guide for configuring agent permissions for Claude Tag! https://t.co/x7G0o8Cjx1

Claude Tag is a paradigm shift in how we ship products at Anthropic. Our internal version merges 65% of product PRs and this is our first product that is natively multi-player and proactive.

We're excited for you to try this out. Let us know your feedback! https://t.co/YOENq4hDSt
Finally, get creative! I'm setting up a "scheduling" channel which lets people tag Claude to find time on our calendars.

When it's working, I ask it to react to the top level thread with the following emojis: ⏲️✅❓🛑

That way it's easy to see its status at a glance.

When you really get going, it's easy to get overwhelmed with all the threads. 

In my personal channel I like to ask Claude to keep a pinned message that it updates with the status of everything.

It starts with a prompt — it’s a whole lot of work to get here — but it starts with an idea.

Congrats Yousef and team! https://t.co/ARUtRHwvFf https://t.co/fu1FQpfyHY

x.name: Aaron Levie
x.bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI

tweets.text:
Good post on the dynamics of AI pricing right now. We’re basically going to see a barbell dynamic on pricing between high cost frontier models and cheap but good open or closed weights models. 

Then the question is how do you maximize efficiency from the frontier models, and get better quality and performance of the cheaper models. That’s where the applied AI layer comes in.

The applied layer can route to the best model at any time given the workload at hand, as well as mitigate either high token costs or worse performance. The closer you are to the underlying workflow, the better you can tune the use of models for a given business process. 

That’s all about understanding the workflows, domain specific FDEs, evals by customer or process, getting data setup well for agents, and more. Overall good for applied AI.

Another example of the power of headless software with agents. With Claude Tag, you can give Claude access to any corporate files in Box that you can interact with from Slack. Now all of your enterprise content becomes a portable knowledge base. https://t.co/m7UPdmqnn8 https://t.co/qrHqoZKHG4

tweets.text:
Built using @conductor_build, @AnthropicAI Opus 4.8, @shopify UCP CLI and @Railway.

Live now here: https://t.co/oSJhkFkEKP

Introducing Plug That Shop 🔌

So @Shopify launched their UCP CLI that allows all the products on their stores available via the command line.

I was thinking of interesting use cases for the CLI over the weekend and realized that I can use this to create contextual embedded shops for websites.

So now, you can enter your website and get four HIGHLY relevant shops that you can embed in just one click.

For the buyer using this shop, it's an insanely simple experience since they can click buy and checkout with Shop in literally one click.

Available, free to use, NO affiliate revenue at plugthatshop dot com

Feedback welcome!

x.name: Claude
x.bio: Claude is an AI assistant built by @anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on https://t.co/ZhTwG8d1e5 or download the app.

tweets.text:
Claude Tag is available in beta today on Slack for Claude Enterprise and Team plans. We plan to expand it more widely in the future.

Read more: https://t.co/MGV30Ml8mL
Turn ambient behavior on, and Claude takes initiative. It follows up on threads that have gone quiet and flags what's relevant from across its channels and tools. https://t.co/fTMkAHY4Gr

In a channel, there’s one Claude that interacts with everyone, so a teammate can pick up exactly where you left off.

It builds more context about the work as it follows the channel, so you don’t need to explain things from scratch.
